note: March at Ahmedabad: 20-36C, summer building fast. Heritage pols and stepwells still walkable early morning; midday outside feels sticky and sharp.
March at Ambaji: 18-33C, pre-summer warm. Temple darshan easy; the hilltop climb is doable but late-morning sun starts to bite.
March at Bhavnagar: 20-35C, humid coastal warmth creeping in. Takhteshwar and museums fine; outdoor heritage best before 10am.
March at Champaner-Pavagadh: 19-35C at base, cooler up top. Climb early, the exposed mosques and fort walls heat up by noon.
March at Chinnar: 20-34C and bone dry, the whole point of coming here. Wildlife concentrates at waterholes; midday treks are hot going.
March at Coimbatore: 22-34C, warming up before the monsoon break. Adiyogi and city fine; Nilgiri day-trips feel better than staying down on the plains.
March at Dakor: 21-36C, pre-summer heat. Ranchhodrai temple darshan workable in cooler hours; midday courtyard walks get harsh.
March at Dharmasthala: 22-34C, humid. Temple queues and annadana run all day; step outside and the Western Ghats humidity is already noticeable.
March at Dholavira: 20-35C, desert heat ramping up. Harappan ruins are open plateau with zero shade, so finish the walk before 10am.
March at Dwarka: 22-33C, sea breeze helps but it's getting sticky. Temple and ghats workable; beach time fine in early morning or evening.
March at Gandhinagar: 20-36C, tree-lined streets help. Akshardham and Adalaj doable, just keep outdoor stretches to morning and dusk.
March at Girnar: 18-34C. The 10,000-step climb is still on, but it's firmly a pre-4am start — by mid-morning the exposed sections are punishing.
March at Halebidu: 19-33C, plateau heat building. Hoysaleshwara's 240 carved panels reward an early morning visit before the stone heats up.
March at Junagadh: 19-35C. Fort, caves and Ashokan edicts are all workable early; midday on the exposed slopes gets uncomfortable.
March at Kukke Subramanya: 22-33C, Western Ghats humid. Temple rituals run normally; Kumara Parvatha trek is hot going and dries out water sources.
March at Kutch: 18-35C, Rann season ending. Craft villages still workable, but days warm fast and the white desert magic from winter has faded.
March at Little Andaman: 25-32C, humid. Butler Bay waves holding; ferry access workable but seas getting choppier as pre-monsoon winds build.
March at Lonar: 20-36C on the Deccan plateau. Crater rim walk fine at sunrise, brutal midday — no shade around the alkaline lake.
March at Lothal: 22-36C. The 4,500-year-old dock is fully exposed brick and packed earth; go early, bring water, skip midday.
March at Margao: 24-34C, humid. Konkani markets and Portuguese lanes workable; beach day-trips feel heavier than the Dec-Feb sweet spot.
March at Meghamalai: 14-26C up top, cooler than plains. Cloud forest workable; access roads dry and dusty before pre-monsoon showers start.
March at Nandi Hills: 16-30C. Sunrise view holds up; the fort climb is fine, but Bangalore weekenders pile in so early arrival matters.
March at Point Calimere: 25-33C, humid coast. Flamingos and blackbucks still around but numbers thinning as the wetland dries into summer.
March at Ponda: 23-34C, Goa humid. Spice plantation walks are shaded so it's workable, but the canopy air is close and heavy by midday.
March at Porbandar: 22-33C, coastal humidity rising. Kirti Mandir and Gandhi sites fine; the beach is better early morning.
March at Raigad: 17-33C. Ropeway or stairs both workable; pre-summer haze dulls the valley views and the summit plateau heats up by noon.
March at Rajkot: 20-36C. Kathiawadi food scene is year-round; heritage walks and Gandhi sites fine early, midday sun is unkind.
March at Patan: 20-36C. The 7-storey stepwell is cool once you descend, but the approach and surrounds bake — plan an early visit.
March at Sakleshpur: 18-31C, coffee country drying out. Railway treks and estate stays workable; forests are crispier and dustier than winter.
March at Saputara: 15-30C, Gujarat's only hill station. Tribal markets and viewpoints still fine; forests are dry and pre-summer haze softens views.
March at Shirdi: 21-36C. Samadhi Mandir darshan runs all day; queue compounds get hot, so aim for morning or late evening slots.
March at Somnath: 22-33C, coastal breeze helps. Jyotirlinga darshan and seafront aarti still work well; just skip the midday temple courtyard walk.
March at Srirangapatna: 22-35C. Fort, Daria Daulat and Ranganathaswamy temple fine early; the Kaveri island breeze fades by noon and sun sharpens.
March at Statue of Unity: 20-36C. Narmada valley views still fine; viewing gallery is AC but queues and approach plaza get hot fast.
March at Surat: 23-36C, sticky. Street food and diamond district run normally; outdoor sightseeing best at dawn or after sunset.
March at Trimbakeshwar: 16-33C. Jyotirlinga darshan and Godavari origin walks workable; afternoons on the exposed kund steps start to heat up.
March at Vadodara: 21-36C. Laxmi Vilas Palace and museums are shaded wins; garden and heritage walking better before 10am.
March at Valparai: 17-29C up in the hills. Tea estates and macaque sightings fine; 40 hairpins dusty before pre-monsoon showers arrive.
March at Vedanthangal: 22-34C. Late nesting birds still around, but numbers dropping as the wetland shrinks towards summer end of season.
April at Alleppey: 26-34C, humid. Houseboats still run but cabin AC gets a workout; canal cruises feel heavier than the Dec-Feb peak.
April at Bekal: 26-34C, humid coastal heat. Fort and beach walks fine early morning; midday on the exposed ramparts is rough.
April at Beypore: 26-33C, sticky. Uru shipyard still building all year; port walks workable in cooler hours, middays on the beach feel heavy.
April at Cherai: 27-33C. Dolphins and Chinese nets still there; beach shack scene quiet, water warm but humidity sits on you all day.
April at Cuttack: 26-40C. Silver filigree workshops and indoor heritage fine; Barabati Fort and open-air walking get brutal midday.
April at Daman: 25-34C, humid. Portuguese forts and churches still worth it; beaches workable dawn and dusk, midday sun is heavy.
April at Deomali: 22-36C at base, 18-28C on summit. Trek doable if you start before dawn; lower sections hot, upper ridge pleasant.
April at Diamond Triangle: 26-39C. Lalitgiri, Ratnagiri and Udayagiri are open-plateau ruins — finish the circuit early, midday heat is punishing.
April at Diu: 25-33C. Island breeze takes the edge off; forts and churches fine, beach days workable early morning and evening.
April at Guruvayur: 26-34C, humid. Temple darshan and elephant sanctuary run normally; the queue galleries feel close and sticky midday.
April at Idukki: 22-32C in the cardamom hills. Dam views and wildlife workable; pre-monsoon showers start late month, roads can turn slick.
April at Kannur: 26-34C. Theyyam season winding down, some performances still on; beaches humid but drive-in sands still drive-able.
April at Karaikal: 27-36C, humid delta heat. Temples and French fort ruins workable early; beach afternoons feel heavy and muggy.
April at Karwar: 26-34C. Devbagh boat trips still running; beaches workable in cooler hours, midday humidity is the main tradeoff.
April at Kashid: 26-34C. White sand still looks great but the sand gets hot fast; swim windows shrink to early morning and late afternoon.
April at Kochi: 26-34C, humid. Chinese nets, Fort Kochi lanes and museums all fine; Biennale over, some venues quieter than peak season.
April at Kollam: 26-34C. Ashtamudi cruises and Munroe Island canoes still run; the long 8-hour Alleppey backwater haul feels longer in humid heat.
April at Kovalam: 26-33C. Lighthouse and Hawa beaches still scenic; Ayurveda centres full year-round; midday sand is hot to walk on.
April at Kozhikode: 26-34C. Malabar biryani doesn't care about weather; heritage and beach walks workable early and late, midday is sticky.
April at Kumarakom: 26-34C. Vembanad cruises and bird sanctuary still running but Siberian migrants have already left; lake heat is heavy midday.
April at Kumbalangi: 26-33C, humid. Crab farms and village life run normally; canoe rides better at dawn or after 4pm.
April at Kuttanad: 26-34C. Below-sea-level paddies still green; boat rides workable, just bring sun cover for the open-canopy stretches.
April at Machilipatnam: 28-38C, humid. Kalamkari workshops run year-round; coastal heritage walks are uncomfortable outside dawn/dusk.
April at Malvan: 26-34C. Scuba and snorkelling still workable before monsoon closes diving in June; Sindhudurg fort breezy but midday sun is strong.
April at Marari: 26-33C. Fishing village rhythm unchanged; beach still quiet, just warmer and stickier than the Dec-Feb sweet spot.
April at Marine National Park: 25-35C. Low-tide coral walks still on, tide charts still the boss; heat builds fast once you're back on open sand.
April at Meghamalai: 15-27C up top. Cloud forest cool and workable; pre-monsoon showers starting, access road can get slick late month.
April at Morjim: 26-33C. Olive ridley nesting season winding down; Russian crowd thinning; beach shacks quiet, heat workable with sea breeze.
April at Munnar: 17-28C. Tea estates still open and cooler than plains; afternoon mist and pre-monsoon showers starting to build.
April at Murudeshwar: 26-34C. Shiva statue and temple fine; snorkelling trips to Netrani still workable but seas getting choppier pre-monsoon.
April at Nagarjuna Sagar: 28-40C. Buddhist museum island cool on the ferry, but the dam walk is fully exposed — don't attempt midday.
April at Nalsarovar: 25-38C. Flamingos mostly gone, wetland shrinking; some resident waders still around but far fewer than Jan-Feb peak.
April at Nelliyampathy: 18-29C. Coffee and tea estates pleasant; roads dusty pre-monsoon and birding action quieter than the wet season.
April at Nrusinghanath-Harishankar: 24-38C at base. Twin temples workable early; the plateau walk between them gets hot with little shade.
April at Ponmudi: 18-28C up the 22 hairpins. Butterflies still active; cool relief from Trivandrum but afternoon showers starting.
April at Poovar: 26-33C. River-sea estuary boat rides still run; island resorts quiet, humidity heavy but shaded stretches workable.
April at Rajahmundry: 28-39C. Godavari cruises still running but deck heat is punishing; heritage city walks need dawn/dusk timing.
April at Reis Magos: 26-33C. Restored fort museum shaded and workable; Mandovi views still good, just hazier than winter and more humid.
April at Sambalpur: 26-40C. Ikat weavers work year-round indoors; Hirakud dam and town walking uncomfortable midday, early starts only.
April at Talasari: 27-37C, humid. Quiet river-meets-sea beach still workable but the sand heats up fast — sunrise and sunset are the windows.
April at Thekkady: 20-32C. Periyar lake boat rides still run; wildlife concentrates near water but afternoon showers can cut trekking short.
April at Trivandrum: 26-34C. Padmanabhaswamy darshan and museums fine; zoo and palace walks workable early, midday feels close and humid.
April at Udupi: 26-34C. Krishna temple and dosa breakfast run year-round; Malpe beach workable in cooler hours, midday sand is hot.
April at Vagamon: 18-28C. Pine meadows and paragliding still on; pre-monsoon afternoon showers starting so launch windows tighten.
April at Valparai: 17-29C in the cloud forest. Still cooler than plains; 40 hairpins dusty and pre-monsoon showers starting to make roads slick.
April at Varkala: 26-33C. Cliff views and yoga scene still on; season winding down, many cafes closing for monsoon by month end.
April at Wayanad: 20-32C. Caves and treks still workable; forests drier than wet season and pre-monsoon showers start mid-month.
September at Aihole: 22-32C. Monsoon winding down, Chalukya temples workable; occasional showers can soften the light but also interrupt walks.
September at Alleppey: 24-30C. Rains still around but easing; houseboats run, canals full, just expect wet afternoons and humid evenings.
September at Beypore: 24-30C. Monsoon retreating but still damp; Uru shipyard works through rain, beach walks are hit-and-miss with showers.
September at Bhavnagar: 25-33C, monsoon ending. Takhteshwar and museums fine; humidity still heavy, outdoor heritage better once skies clear mid-month.
September at Bidar: 21-31C. Monsoon retreating on the Deccan plateau; Bahmani fort and Bidriware workshops workable, just expect muddy approaches.
September at Champaner-Pavagadh: 23-32C. Monsoon greening up the hills; climb can be slick after rain, mosques and temples still accessible.
September at Chitradurga: 21-31C. Seven-walled fort workable between showers; boulder paths slick when wet, carry grip shoes.
September at Coimbatore: 22-32C. Monsoon fading, Nilgiri gateway still damp; Adiyogi and city fine, day-trips up the ghats can hit leftover showers.
September at Daman: 24-31C. Monsoon retreat underway; Portuguese forts workable, beaches still grey and humid but openings between showers.
September at Dharmasthala: 22-30C, Western Ghats still wet. Temple and annadana run rain-or-shine; outdoor approaches slick, carry an umbrella.
September at Diu: 25-32C, Arabian Sea humid. Island workable as monsoon eases; forts and churches fine, beaches still have rough-swell days.
September at Dwarka: 26-32C. Monsoon retreating on the Saurashtra coast; Char Dham darshan workable, sea still choppy on some days.
September at Gandhinagar: 24-32C. Monsoon fading, gardens at their greenest; Akshardham and Adalaj workable between showers.
September at Gulbarga: 21-31C. Bahmani fort, mosque and Sufi shrines workable; monsoon easing so the plateau is green but paths still muddy.
September at Guruvayur: 24-30C. Temple and elephant sanctuary run through rain; darshan queues are covered but approaches get puddled.
September at Junagadh: 24-32C. Caves and fort workable, Girnar's lower reaches greener than winter; upper climb can be slick after rain.
September at Kannur: 24-30C. Theyyam season not yet started; Portuguese forts and drive-in beach workable between showers.
September at Kochi: 24-30C. Monsoon retreating; Fort Kochi lanes, Chinese nets and museums all workable, just carry an umbrella.
September at Kollam: 24-30C. Ashtamudi cruises and Munroe canoes run; backwaters full and green, showers interrupt but rarely cancel.
September at Kozhikode: 24-30C. Malabar food scene unaffected by weather; heritage and beach walks doable between rain bands.
September at Kumarakom: 24-30C. Vembanad full, bird sanctuary quiet before Siberian migrants arrive; cruises run but verandah views often wet.
September at Kumbalangi: 24-30C. Crab farms and village life carry on through rain; canoe rides wet but workable, scenery lush.
September at Kuttanad: 24-30C. Paddies submerged deeper than usual; houseboat rides dramatic but wet, dawn breaks occasionally clear.
September at Lothal: 24-33C. Harappan dock ruins workable as monsoon eases; open site, so time visits around cleared skies, not drizzle.
September at Mandvi: 26-33C. Vijay Vilas and dhow yards workable; beach grey and breezy as Kutch monsoon retreats, swimming still not great.
September at Margao: 24-30C. Markets, churches and Goan lunch all work in light rain; heavy downpour days still happen but spacing out.
September at Meghamalai: 14-24C. Cloud forest at its wettest-lushest; access road prone to slips, bookings and drivers worth double-confirming.
September at Nrusinghanath-Harishankar: 23-32C. Temples open, forest green after monsoon; pilgrim circuit reopening cautiously, trails still slick.
September at Poovar: 24-30C. Estuary backwaters full, boat-access resorts running; beach days hit-and-miss between showers.
September at Rajkot: 24-33C. Monsoon winding down; Kathiawadi food unaffected, Gandhi sites and heritage walks workable between showers.
September at Patan: 24-32C. Stepwell interior is always cool; monsoon green softens the approach but puddled in stretches, skies clearing mid-month.
September at Reis Magos: 24-30C. Restored fort museum shaded and rain-friendly; Mandovi views often grey, Goa still emptying of monsoon.
September at Shirdi: 22-30C. Samadhi Mandir darshan normal; town workable between showers, monsoon fading so queues more comfortable than peak summer.
September at Somnath: 25-31C. Jyotirlinga darshan and sea-front aarti run rain-or-shine; swell still rough some days, beach walks weather-dependent.
September at Statue of Unity: 23-32C. Narmada full and scenic; viewing gallery fine, but open plaza and boat rides can get rained off.
September at Surat: 25-33C. Monsoon fading, diamond district and food scene run normally; outdoor sightseeing still weather-dependent.
September at Thrissur: 23-30C. Pooram is long over but temples and heritage lanes fine; showers still frequent, sound-and-light events weather-dependent.
September at Trimbakeshwar: 19-29C. Godavari in full flow, kund rituals can be crowded; rain breaks frequent, carry grip shoes for the ghats.
September at Trivandrum: 24-30C. Padmanabhaswamy darshan and museums fine; monsoon retreating so outdoor stretches between showers open up.
September at Valparai: 17-26C. Cloud forest at its wettest — macaques and leeches both active; 40 hairpins slick, drivers cautious.
